text: The divine nature and eternal existence of Christ are here declared. He is the Word, with God, and is God, the fountain of life and light.
John the Baptist is introduced as a witness to the Light. Christ comes into the world, though the world knew Him not. Yet to those who receive Him, He gives power to become children of God.
John bears witness to Christ’s superiority. The fullness of grace and truth is found in Him.
Christ is revealed as the Lamb of God. The first disciples are called and begin to follow Him.
The miracle at Cana shows Christ’s power over nature and His approval of marriage.
Christ cleanses the temple, demonstrating zeal for God's house and authority over worship.
Christ teaches Nicodemus the necessity of being born again by the Spirit.
Christ explains salvation and God's love for the world in giving His Son.
John the Baptist exalts Christ, declaring that He must increase.
Christ reveals Himself to the Samaritan woman and offers living water.
Many Samaritans believe through the woman's testimony and Christ’s word.
Christ heals the nobleman’s son, showing the power of faith.
Christ heals at Bethesda and declares His authority equal with the Father.
Christ teaches about His divine authority, judgment, and witness.
Christ feeds the five thousand and walks on water.
Christ declares Himself the Bread of Life.
Many disciples turn away, but Peter confesses Christ.
Jesus goes to the Feast of Booths amid opposition and secrecy.
Christ teaches in the temple with divine authority.
Debate arises about Christ’s identity and origin.
Christ invites all to come to Him for living water.
The woman caught in adultery is shown mercy.
Christ declares Himself the Light of the world.
Christ teaches truth, freedom, and His eternal existence.
Christ heals a man born blind.
The Pharisees question the miracle.
Spiritual blindness is exposed.
Christ is the Good Shepherd who lays down His life.
Christ declares unity with the Father.
Lazarus dies and Christ declares Himself the resurrection.
Christ raises Lazarus from the dead.
Mary anoints Jesus and He enters Jerusalem.
Christ speaks of His death and calls for faith.
Christ washes the disciples’ feet.
Christ foretells betrayal and denial.
Christ comforts His disciples and declares Himself the way.
Promise of the Holy Spirit and peace.
Christ is the true vine; believers must abide in Him.
The world hates Christ’s followers.
The Holy Spirit will guide believers into truth.
Christ speaks of sorrow turning to joy and His victory.
Christ’s high priestly prayer for His people.
Christ is arrested and Peter denies Him.
Christ stands before Pilate.
Christ is crucified and dies.
Christ is buried.
Christ rises from the dead and appears to Mary.
Christ appears to His disciples and Thomas believes.
Christ appears by the sea and provides a miraculous catch.
Christ restores Peter and commissions him.
